reasoning: Silhouette is solid — the arch reads instantly at 1x on grey, and the gold frame + dark green portal interior create a clear open-gate read. Edge cleanliness is good for pixel art; no mushy bleed. On dark bg the gold/green contrast is strong and inviting. On light/cream bg the warm gold mid-tones blend into the background more than ideal — the shape still reads but loses pop. Charm is present (the teal gem cap at the arch apex is a nice personality touch, and the warm amber frame has a toylike warmth) but the overall aesthetic skews 'elegant temple gate' rather than 'friendly toy' — the upright rectangular proportions and deep dark interior feel slightly imposing rather than soft/round/approachable. At 1x the gem is lost entirely, reducing personality. Passes silhouette and dual-bg legibility thresholds, but the cute/charm dimension is only partial — it would benefit from slightly softer arch shoulders and a brighter gem that survives 1x.
